Budd-Chiari syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by occlusion of the hepatic veins and typically presents with abdominal pain, ascites and hepatomegaly.
Budd-Chiari syndrome.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; asterixis; mild encephalopathy
Budd-Chiari syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; moderate to severe encephalopathy; coma

Hepatic failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by the inability of the liver to metabolize chemicals in the body. Laboratory test results reveal abnormal plasma levels of ammonia, bilirubin, lactic dehydrogenase, alkaline phosphatase, aminotransferase, and/or prolongation of prothrombin time (INR.) Drug-induced liever injury (DILI) as defined by Hy's Law.
Hepatic failure.Grade 3: Asterixis; mild encephalopathy; drug-induced liver injury (DILI); limiting self care ADL
Hepatic failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; moderate to severe encephalopathy; coma

Hepatic hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the liver.
Hepatic hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization

Hepatic necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the hepatic parenchyma.
Hepatic necrosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated

Alanine aminotransferase increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an increase in the level of alanine aminotransferase (ALT or SGPT) in the blood specimen.
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 3.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; 1.5 - 3.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 2: >3.0 - 5.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >3.0 - 5.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 3: >5.0 - 20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >5.0 - 20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alanine aminotransferase increased.Grade 4: >20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal

Alkaline phosphatase increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an increase in the level of alkaline phosphatase in a blood specimen.
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 2.5 x ULN if baseline was normal; 2.0 - 2.5 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 2: >2.5 - 5.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >2.5 - 5.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 3: >5.0 - 20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >5.0 - 20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Alkaline phosphatase increased.Grade 4: >20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal

Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an increase in the level of aspartate aminotransferase (AST or SGOT) in a blood specimen.
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 3.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; 1.5 - 3.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 2: >3.0 - 5.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >3.0 - 5.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 3: >5.0 - 20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >5.0 - 20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Aspartate aminotransferase increased.Grade 4: >20.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >20.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal

Blood bilirubin increased.Definition: A finding based on laboratory test results that indicate an abnormally high level of bilirubin in the blood. Excess bilirubin is associated with jaundice.
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 1: >ULN - 1.5 x ULN if baseline was normal; > 1.0 - 1.5 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 2: >1.5 - 3.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >1.5 - 3.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 3: >3.0 - 10.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >3.0 - 10.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
Blood bilirubin increased.Grade 4: >10.0 x ULN if baseline was normal; >10.0 x baseline if baseline was abnormal
